# Quantum Vector Labs (QVL) - LLM Documentation

This file serves as a comprehensive guide for AI agents and LLMs to understand the architecture, stack, and intent of the QVL project.

## 1. Project Identity
- **Name:** Quantum Vector Labs
- **Domain:** qvlabs.ai
- **Purpose:** Marketing and operational platform for a GenAI infrastructure provider.
- **Philosophy:** Files over Frameworks. Intelligence as Code.

## 2. Core Technical Stack
- **Framework:** Next.js 16 (App Router)
- **Runtime:** Node.js (React 19)
- **Language:** TypeScript 5
- **Styling:** TailwindCSS 3.4 (Custom Cyberpunk Theme)
- **Authentication:** Stack Auth (@stackframe/stack)
- **AI Inference:** Vultr Serverless Inference (OpenAI-compatible)
- **AI SDK:** Vercel AI SDK v6 (Streaming)
- **Database (planned):** Neon DB (Postgres)

## 3. Key Architectural Concepts

### Leader Brain
A file-native intelligence layer stored in the `leader-brain/` directory. It uses Markdown files to provide persistent memory, personality, and context to AI agents. It avoids vendor lock-in by treating "System Prompts as a Knowledge Base."

### AXON (Autonomous eXecution and Orchestration Network)
The multi-agent execution layer that operates on the Leader Brain. It orchestrates specialised agents (Planner, Executor, Critic, Memory) to fulfill "Dreams" (tasks).

### AI Chatbot (Sidebar)
A floating assistant powered by `/api/chat`. It uses manual SSE stream parsing instead of abstraction hooks for maximum control and transparency.

## 4. Directory Structure Map
- `app/`: Next.js App Router (Pages & API Routes). Includes `(auth)`, `(main)`, and `(user)` route groups.
- `components/`: React components (home, layout, auth)
- `lib/`: Shared utilities (auth hooks, brain API, mddb)
- `stack/`: Stack Auth client/server configurations
- `leader-brain/`: The "Memory" of the system (Markdown files)
- `public/`: Static assets and OpenGraph images
- `stack/`: Auth configuration

## 5. Development Patterns
- **Styling:** Use custom utility classes: `.glass-panel`, `.vector-glow`, `.card-ring`, `.text-gradient`.
- **Auth:** Use the `useAuth()` hook from `lib/auth-context.tsx`.
- **Inference:** Route all LLM requests through `/api/chat` (Vultr Inference).
- **Metadata:** SEO is managed in `layout.tsx` files using the Next.js Metadata API.

## 6. Key Routes
- `/` — Marketing landing page.
- `/auth` — Sign in / sign up.
- `/settings` — User settings (General, Account, Privacy, Billing).
- `/learn` — Documentation and concept deep-dives.
- `/pricing` — Service tiers and scaling options.
- `/solutions` — Industry-specific AI solutions.
- `not-found` — Custom 404 error page.

## 7. Authentication (Stack Auth)
The project has migrated from Supabase to Stack Auth. 
- **Client App:** `stack/client.ts`
- **Server App:** `stack/server.ts`
- **Handler:** `app/handler/[...stack]/page.tsx`
- **Context:** `lib/auth-context.tsx` provides a compatibility layer for existing components.

## 7. SEO & Metadata
- **Base URL:** https://qvlabs.ai
- **Sitemap:** Dynamic at `/sitemap.xml`
- **Robots:** Configured at `/robots.txt`
- **OG Images:** Located in `/public/og/`

## 8. Critical Constraints
- Do not modify `.env.local` via agents without explicit permission.
- Always maintain the dark theme (`forced dark mode`).
- Ensure all new features are documented in the Leader Brain project entry.
- Follow the "Research -> Strategy -> Execution" workflow.

---
*Documentation for LLMs v1.0.0 - 2026-03-29*
